name: Hydrological vs Terrain Maps
description: The knowledge database reveals two exclusive map sets: one pinpoints a region with exceptional water reserves, the other marks stable, fertile ground. Which route should the caravan prioritise?

effects.message: The hydrological charts guide the caravan toward a pristine watershed. The next region's water supply will be far safer.
Ancient survey data reveals a stretch of stable ground. The next area's land quality will be much more forgiving.

name: Water vs Climate
description: The database holds conflicting forecasts: one predicts a region with abundant fresh water, the other promises mild temperatures. Only one lead can be pursued.

effects.message: The caravan diverts toward the predicted spring line, ensuring the next location's water danger drops sharply.
Climate models pinpoint a pocket of gentle weather ahead. The next stop's temperature will be far less extreme.

name: Water vs Rainfall
description: Two promising predictions: a hidden aquifer or a zone of steady, gentle rain. The caravan must decide which asset matters more right now.

effects.message: The aquifer's location is uploaded to the nav system. Next region's water supply will be significantly safer.
Chasing the rain band promises a well-watered next stop. Precipitation danger will be notably reduced.

name: Water vs Flora
description: A fork in the data: one path leads to a chain of clear springs, the other to dense, edible vegetation. Which provision should the caravan secure?

effects.message: The spring chain is locked in. The next location's water supply will be much less hazardous.
The database's flora scan leads to a lush region ahead. Vegetation danger plummets for the next stop.

name: Water vs Radiation
description: The science terminal flags a clean lake, but also a valley with unusually low background radiation. Only one destination can be reached before nightfall.

effects.message: The clean lake coordinates are set. The next region's water supply will be dramatically safer.
The valley's low radiation signature guides the way. Next stop's radiation threat drops substantially.

name: Water vs Shelter
description: Two promising sites: a riverbend with drinkable water, or the ruins of a bunker that would provide sturdy shelter. Which necessity comes first?

effects.message: The riverbend's coordinates promise relief. Water supply danger in the next area is greatly reduced.
The bunker's silhouette appears on the map. The next location's shelter will be far more secure.

name: Land vs Climate
description: The database highlights two safe havens: one with rich, tillable soil, the other with consistently comfortable temperatures. The caravan must choose its next camp.

effects.message: The fertile ground data reshapes the route. The next region's land will be far easier to work.
The temperate zone becomes the new target. Next location's temperature danger falls sharply.

name: Land vs Rainfall
description: Two terrain analyses: one predicts solid, arable soil; the other suggests consistent rain that will keep crops alive. Which advantage to chase?

effects.message: The arable soil prediction reshapes the caravan's path. The next stop's land danger decreases markedly.
Steady rain models guide the caravan forward. Precipitation danger next area is significantly lower.

name: Land vs Vegetation
description: The scouts’ data reveals a stretch of perfect farmland, while another report speaks of wild orchards. Only one location can be reached with the caravan’s remaining fuel.

effects.message: The farmland's bearing is fed into the nav system. Land danger next move will be greatly reduced.
Orchard coordinates override the current heading. The next region's vegetation danger drops sharply.

name: Land vs Radiation
description: A terrain scan shows a perfect building site, but a radiation survey marks a clean zone nearby. Only one gamble can be made.

effects.message: The building site's stability is confirmed. Next location's land threat will be much less severe.
The clean zone's readings reroute the caravan. Radiation danger next stop will plummet.

name: Land vs Shelter
description: Two opportunities: a wide, clear area ideal for farming, or a canyon riddled with natural caves. Which does the caravan need more?

effects.message: The farming area's soil analysis boosts confidence. The next region's land danger is substantially lower.
The canyon's cave network looks promising. Next location's shelter will be far more reliable.

name: Climate vs Rainfall
description: Predictive models disagree: one points to a region of stable, pleasant temperatures; the other promises ample rainfall. Only one projection can be followed.

effects.message: The temperature model adjusts the caravan's heading. Next stop's climate will be far more tolerable.
The rainfall projection steers the group towards a wetter, safer zone. Precipitation danger decreases sharply.

name: Climate vs Vegetation
description: Two climate‑based advantages: mild weather that would ease travel, or a microclimate where edible plants flourish. The caravan must pick its priority.

effects.message: The mild weather corridor is now the target. Temperature danger at the next site drops substantially.
The microclimate's plant life promises bounty. Vegetation threat next move will be greatly reduced.

name: Climate vs Radiation
description: One projection shows a comfortable climate, but a rival scan has found a pocket with nearly zero radiation. The caravan can only travel towards one.

effects.message: The comfort zone's coordinates override the map. Next region's temperature will be much safer.
The radiation pocket's beacon is locked in. The next location's radiation danger will be minimal.

name: Climate vs Shelter
description: Two scientific predictions: a stretch of warm, easy‑going weather, or a region full of natural shelters. Only one can be reached with current supplies.

effects.message: The warm zone prediction shifts the caravan's route. Next site's temperature threat falls dramatically.
The region of shelters appears on the scanner. The next location's shelter will be exceptionally safe.

name: Rainfall vs Vegetation
description: The database suggests two fertile zones: one defined by regular, nourishing rain; the other by thick, natural plant cover. Which resource should guide the caravan?

effects.message: The rain charts become the new guide. The next area's precipitation danger will be far less worrying.
The thick vegetation data points to a safer stop. Vegetation threat next move drops substantially.

name: Rainfall vs Radiation
description: A hard choice from the models: a rain‑rich area that will keep water barrels full, or a sanctuary with almost no radiation signature. One must be sacrificed.

effects.message: Choosing the rain-rich area ensures a well-hydrated next stop. Precipitation danger falls sharply.
The radiation-free sanctuary is set as the next waypoint. Radiation danger there will be nearly nonexistent.

name: Rainfall vs Shelter
description: Two analysed locations: one benefits from constant light rain that can be harvested; the other offers strong, pre‑existing structures. The caravan must decide.

effects.message: The rain zone's forecast is plugged into the route. Next area's precipitation danger will be markedly lower.
The pre-existing structures become the caravan's destination. Shelter quality next stop will be greatly improved.

name: Vegetation vs Radiation
description: The science logs show a forest bursting with food, but also a sterile plateau free of radiation. Only one haven can be reached with the remaining daylight.

effects.message: The forest's biomass signature promises safety. Next location's vegetation danger will be drastically reduced.
The plateau's rad-free reading steers the caravan. Radiation threat next stop will be negligible.

name: Vegetation vs Shelter
description: Two encouraging reports: an overgrown valley where food is plentiful, and a cliffside with deep, weather‑proof caves. The caravan can only secure one.

effects.message: The overgrown valley is locked into the route. Next stop's vegetation danger will be far less of a threat.
The cliffside caves promise immense shelter. The next location's shelter quality will be exceptional.

name: Radiation vs Shelter
description: A final dilemma from the database: a region with virtually no radiation, or the remains of a fortified bunker that could house the entire caravan. Only one path can be taken.

effects.message: The clean region's coordinates light up the map. The next stop's radiation danger will be almost wiped out.
The fortified bunker's position is saved. Next location's shelter will be robust and secure.
